title: Cap Rate Calculator
metaTitle: Cap Rate Calculator — Free Real Estate Investor Tool
metaDescription: Calculate cap rate for any rental property in 10 seconds. Enter price, rent, and expenses — no signup required.
tagline: Enter your numbers and see the cap rate instantly.

faq.question: What is a good cap rate for rental property?
faq.answer: Most investors target 5–10%. Below 5% is common in high-cost coastal markets where appreciation is the thesis. Above 10% often signals higher vacancy risk or deferred maintenance — run the numbers carefully.

faq.question: Does cap rate include mortgage payments?
faq.answer: No. Cap rate uses Net Operating Income divided by purchase price. NOI is pre-financing — it excludes mortgage principal and interest. That's intentional: it lets you compare properties independent of how each one is financed.

faq.question: What expenses go into the operating expense ratio?
faq.answer: Property taxes, insurance, maintenance, property management fees (typically 8–12%), vacancy allowance (5–10%), and reserves for capital expenditures. A 40–50% expense ratio is a reasonable starting assumption for a single-family rental.

faq.question: How is cap rate different from cash-on-cash return?
faq.answer: Cap rate ignores financing. Cash-on-cash return accounts for your actual loan payments and measures your annual cash flow against the cash you put in. Use cap rate to compare deals; use cash-on-cash to evaluate your actual return given your specific financing.
